WT: 4.0 Size: TINY

Race: ALL

AC: 10

# Apr 15 2002 at 4:28 PM Rating: Default

8 posts
A trio of low 50s, a duo of mid 50s, a solo char of high level (and a soloing class).

56 Druid and 57 Paly worked well, for example.

Necromancers solo it earlier than most other classes.

# Dec 29 2001 at 7:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249933838
Scholar
46 posts
The Price of this is now going to drop quick once a few of these new items get out on the market.

Shiny Trinket of Speed
LORE ITEM MAGIC ITEM
AC: +25 Str: +15 Sta: +10 Dex: +10 HP: +25
Weight: 0.1
Classes: Warrior Rogue Monk Bard Shadowknight Paladin Ranger Beastlord
Races: All Races
Inventory Slot: Ranged Weapon

this new item owns the idol in every way. I expect the idol to start selling for about 5k or a little less when this gets out on the market. When the Shiny Trinket gets on the market expect a price of at least 15k. when the price of the idol drops this will drop some as well. I have seen the idol go for 6k - 10k lately.

Good idol
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2001 at 8:06 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757938532
2 posts
Okay, until velious this was best range item in game, period. for those newbies who post here saying it's not worth it, or that it's just an okay item, you obviously don't understand the most important stat in the game for any melee is AC. AC = most important, and since this item has the most ac (beside some rare velious stuff), it's a VERY good range slot, and very much worth the cost or time to get one.

My exp
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2001 at 9:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591752317205
3 posts
I just got this little bad boy after a guildie got it in 2 hrs.

I was there 8 hrs, guildie held camp 1hr before i showed up. During that time loot from chief was as follows:

6 stickers
2 Essence of Dol
1 Idol

A couple other people i know said they usually got idol after 7 stickers and 3 dols. Had no prob holding this camp with a 53 rake and 51 vicar. Getting there can be a pain if eveyone does not have invis though.
▲ Page top
got this easily
QuoteReply
# Sep 02 2001 at 8:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759537286
2 posts
Went on a guild raid (only 6people showed up) in Droga and we went straight to chief he was up, pulled him and dropped essence and eye? and then we stayed for another pop and he dropped the idol. Won the roll so i am a very happy warrior. He isnt to hard to get to. Just make sure you have a map and go very slowly, we never had any adds. And no one came close to dieing. So all in all it was a very easy camp.
▲ Page top

dam
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2001 at 7:19 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591758644668
157 posts
This guy takes 20mins to spawn its either him or PH. Idol dropped in umm like 20 spawns. Chief does not see true hide. As a magie its real easy, just be around 54 + with epics its just so easy. But without earth is the only choice and will finsih the normal gobo's up. The chief was yellow to a 37 4x. Only other yellow was the maddend burynai dropped gi once i think. Ended up rotting 4 dols 2 circlets and some fs stuff.
▲ Page top
RE: dam
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2001 at 5:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756476296
26 posts
I don't think you need to be a mage with epic to camp this guy. In fact I bet a 50 or lower mage could easily solo this guy. The problem is getting there safely.

Korinn
▲ Page top
RE: dam
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 1:50 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769194019
6 posts
You are very correct. As a 53 mage using the 51 earth pet can wipe up this whole zone. Just clear the way single pulling the gobbies, if you get an add or 2 no big deal at all. Two applications of scintillation and the adds are gone. Even if 4 or 5 gobbies get on pet is no big deal at all, Jabober is not scared. Do the same to the chief room, single pull them all out to front of room in hall and let Jabober go to work. For a mage I think it is much safer and easier to kill your way to a camp rather than invis there, if invis should drop in a bad spot we have no means of escape save gate and it is a really rare time where you can get a pet off without being interrupted of beat down. From what I have seen in EQ, mages are pretty much the kings (and queens) of greenie dungeon farming, hehe. I pull these little buggers with level one nuke so I am never using mana except for burnout and shield of lava for pet so that I have plenty of mana for scintillations if a drastic situation should arise.
▲ Page top
chief room 2
QuoteReply
# Aug 03 2001 at 2:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755682992
37 posts
Went back again to try my luck. First chief spawned with the idol, I was very suprised after my last 13 hour expedition.

Here is some more info that might help:

1) It is very possible to single pull all of the mobs when you are 53+. Use a ranged attack to pull, not proximity.

2) The mobs run, so you must have a snarer. If the mobs run, they don't bring friends back with them. So pet users, have that /pet back off hotkeyed so your pet doesn't cause the place to come down on you if your snare runs out at a bad time.

3) If the chief has nothing in either hand, you just lucked out :)
▲ Page top
GOT IT!
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2001 at 4:34 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I camped the Chief for 29 hours straight and the idol finally dropped. Well worth the wait!
▲ Page top
chief room
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 12:50 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591755682992
37 posts
Was there for 13 hours. None dropped. Person that took over the camp was there for 7 hours. Also none dropped.

A 55 SK can hold the room once broken quite easily. I had to leave when I ran out of bone chips and bandaids. Glad I did, since another 7 hours wouldn't have produced results.

The room has 3 static spawns and 1 wanderer. You can invis and go to the ramp outside of the room. The wanderer can be pulled single but I got a couple adds from the surrounding area (which is why it is hard to break solo). The next 2 came in pairs both times I tried, so I HT'd one to kill it and FD'd to spread spawn time. Single pulled the other one, waited a bit, and rushed the room to take out the last spawn. If you do it right you only have to fight a mob about every 5 min. I then set up a pet in the middle of the room to take the HT's when the SK mobs spawned.
Chief/PH seemed to pop about every 20 mins.
The Chief sees through invis.
Incredibly boring camp. Bring someone in your guild to PL to have some company.
▲ Page top
2 1/2 hours
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 2:54 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
57th necro was camping item and i told him to give me a tell before he left. Friend (51 druid) and I (53 warrior) took over camp. 2 1/2 hours later i had an idol. the necro said he had camped for 6-7 hrs with no drop.

#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 22 2001 at 5:41 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Our guild on Luclin, Norrath's Justice, did this guy yesterday. First Cheify kill, Idol. Hehehe, I got to hold it for rolling, that was NICE. Next time we killed him he had the stickler, then, about 4 peeps which were the backbone of the raid went LD and didn't come back on.......long CR. Hehehe, congrats to Symthicus who won the idol

# Feb 24 2001 at 2:57 PM Rating: Decent

Scholar
22 posts
I got one this afternoon with my Shaman and me. Killed the Chief 16 times. The Sols and Stickers dropped bout half and half (so 7 and 8). Chief spawns on platform in south corner where torch is up on the wall. Took us 10 or 11 hours to do 16 chiefs. Hellish camp, but there are worse.

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 01 2001 at 8:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) WOOT PHAT LEWTZ!!!!! WOOT! btw.. Droga is a great place to farm.. it's underused the most I've ever seen in the place is around 6 ppl.. and they were actually getting exp (wow!) but every other time I've been there it's been me... or me and partner.. or me and my group.. whatever ;) very underused so who cares if you farm? no one is there to complain
